yepplayerprofile.morrigan1: \fs[23]\c[1]Born in the royal castle of Valenka City, Morrigan Blackpearl's early life was shaped by tragedy. At the tender age of two, she and her
yepplayerprofile.morrigan2: \fs[23]\c[1]father fled the city after the death of her mother in the Four-Kingdom war, a wound that left Valenka forever tainted in her eyes.
yepplayerprofile.morrigan3: \fs[23]\c[1]Her father’s somber recounting of those events painted the city in darkness, and it became a place of broken memories for her.
yepplayerprofile.morrigan4: \fs[23]\c[3]Raised in the quiet, sun-dappled Haravi Town, Morrigan grew into a woman of fierce independence. Her spirit refused to be bound by the sorrow
yepplayerprofile.morrigan5: \fs[23]\c[3]of the past. Confident and driven by a strong sense of free will, she found solace in the open skies and bustling markets of Haravi. Sociable
yepplayerprofile.morrigan7: \fs[23]\c[3]by nature, her quick wit and sharp humor drew people to her, though her ambitions often revealed a streak of greed she skillfully masked behind
yepplayerprofile.morrigan9: \fs[23]\c[17]As she matured, Morrigan sought a fresh start away from her father’s protective gaze, moving to Summerbreeze Town. Here, she founded The
yepplayerprofile.morrigan10: \fs[23]\c[17]Eclipse, a powerful organization born from her desire to control her own fate and chart a new path in a world full of shifting alliances and
yepplayerprofile.morrigan11: \fs[23]\c[17]dangerous ambitions. Beneath her sociable exterior lies a woman determined to rise above her past and seize the opportunities life presents.
yepplayerprofile.lilith1: \fs[23]\c[1]Lilith Harventon was born into the heart of Haravi Town, the cherished daughter of a miner family. Her early years were filled with the warmth of
yepplayerprofile.lilith2: \fs[23]\c[1]her’ parents’ love and the simplicity of their humble life. However, this happiness was shattered when a tragic accident in the town's coal mine took
yepplayerprofile.lilith3: \fs[23]\c[1]both her parents from her at a young age.
yepplayerprofile.lilith4: \fs[23]\c[3]Orphaned and alone, Lilith was taken in by the local orphanage, where she was raised under the watchful care of Father Albert. It was there she met
yepplayerprofile.lilith5: \fs[23]\c[3]Morrigan Blackpearl, forming a bond that would later define their paths. Three years ago, in a twist of fate, Lilith was accidentally infused with
yepplayerprofile.lilith6: \fs[23]\c[3]Voodoo Energy, a power that has since become a crucial asset during her travels with Morrigan, especially after the formation of The Eclipse guild.
yepplayerprofile.lilith7: \fs[23]\c[17]Lilith is known for her calm demeanor, sharp intellect, and gentle heart, though her serious nature often leads her to overthink. Deep inside,
yepplayerprofile.lilith8: \fs[23]\c[17]she holds onto an unreachable dream—an impossible longing to see her parents once more. Despite this, Lilith remains a steadfast force within The
yepplayerprofile.lilith9: \fs[23]\c[17]Eclipse, her quiet strength guiding her through the darkness.
yepplayerprofile.blanc1: \fs[23]\c[1]Born in the mysterious and hidden realm of Whitehaven, Blanc Silverhawk is a proud member of the elusive Griffinian race. Her tribe has long guarded
yepplayerprofile.blanc2: \fs[23]\c[1]its isolation,shrouding their home with treacherous sandstorms and quicksand to keep the human world at bay. Blanc grew up in this secluded haven,
yepplayerprofile.blanc3: \fs[23]\c[1]living peacefully with her parents and beloved younger sister, Plati.
yepplayerprofile.blanc4: \fs[23]\c[3]Three years ago, driven by curiosity and a yearning to see the human world, Blanc made a fateful decision. She led Plati beyond the tribe’s protective
yepplayerprofile.blanc5: \fs[23]\c[3]barriers. But in an unexpected twist, Plati was abducted before Blanc's very eyes—leaving her helpless and consumed by guilt.
yepplayerprofile.blanc6: \fs[23]\c[17]Taciturn and thoughtful, Blanc possesses extraordinary strength, a gift of her Griffinian heritage. Though she is often reserved, her emotions can
yepplayerprofile.blanc7: \fs[23]\c[17]lead her to moments of violence. Now, her sole mission is to find Plati and bring her back to the safety of their hidden home, where they can once
yepplayerprofile.blanc8: \fs[23]\c[17]again live in peace. Driven by a mix of remorse and determination, Blanc’s journey is far from over.
yepplayerprofile.leonardo1: \fs[23]\c[1]Born into the esteemed Hargreave family of Valenka City, Leonardo Hargreave was destined for greatness. As the only son of two of Commander
yepplayerprofile.leonardo2: \fs[23]\c[1]Amelia Blackpearl’s most trusted disciples and bodyguards, he shared a close bond with the Blackpearl family. Under Amelia’s tutelage, Leonardo
yepplayerprofile.leonardo3: \fs[23]\c[1]mastered natural magic at an exceptionally young age.
yepplayerprofile.leonardo4: \fs[23]\c[3]However, his world was torn apart during the Four-Kingdom War. At just ten years old, he witnessed the brutal deaths of his parents and his revered
yepplayerprofile.leonardo5: \fs[23]\c[3]mentor, Commander Amelia, at the hands of the Merinberg Kingdom. The tragedy consumed him with hatred, and he cast aside his aspirations of
yepplayerprofile.leonardo6: \fs[23]\c[3]becoming a wizard of justice. Instead, Leonardo left Valenka City, wandering the lands with Amelia’s mighty staff, driven by a singular, burning
yepplayerprofile.leonardo7: \fs[23]\c[3]purpose: to find Barbarius Heimdal, the architect of Valenka’s destruction, and exact his revenge.
yepplayerprofile.leonardo8: \fs[23]\c[17]Though sharp-witted and deeply loyal to the Blackpearl family, Leonardo’s sense of humor has taken a strange, often unnerving turn. His once bright
yepplayerprofile.leonardo9: \fs[23]\c[17]future as a wizard now lies in ruin, replaced by a relentless pursuit of vengeance, with nothing but the echoes of his past guiding him forward.
yepplayerprofile.aiko1: \fs[23]\c[1]Once the radiant Goddess of the Sun and Watcher of Valkadia, Aiko Kusuda's divine duty was shattered when her twin sister, Aina, the Goddess of
yepplayerprofile.aiko2: \fs[23]\c[1]Night, mysteriously vanished. In a desperate search for answers, Aiko reincarnated herself as a demigod in the world of Cindrivia, setting aside her
yepplayerprofile.aiko3: \fs[23]\c[1]godhood to uncover the truth. With the support of loyal allies, she eventually rescued her sister and restored balance to Cindrivia, but not without
yepplayerprofile.aiko4: \fs[23]\c[1]consequence. An evil entity escaped their grasp, fleeing to the world of Valkadia.
yepplayerprofile.aiko5: \fs[23]\c[3]In the wake of this event, the last fragment of the true Goddess of the Sun left Aiko’s body, merging with her sister to open a portal to Valkadia. With
yepplayerprofile.aiko6: \fs[23]\c[3]the godly essence gone, Aiko was left mortal, though the immense divine power of her demigod form still resides within her. Accompanied by her
yepplayerprofile.aiko7: \fs[23]\c[3]childhood friend, Lucas Shadowrath, Aiko entered Valkadia, bearing the heavy responsibility of hunting down the escaped entity.
yepplayerprofile.aiko8: \fs[23]\c[17]Once a carefree and playful soul known for her light-hearted antics, the years of loss and hardship have transformed Aiko into a figure of wisdom
yepplayerprofile.aiko9: \fs[23]\c[17]and grace, embodying the maturity of the former Goddess of the Sun. Now, as a mortal with the heart of a goddess, she stands poised to confront
yepplayerprofile.lucas1: \fs[23]\c[1]Born in the icy town of Snow Halation, Lucas Shadowrath grew up under the legacy of his father, the legendary treasure hunter Jason Shadowrath. His
yepplayerprofile.lucas2: \fs[23]\c[1]life took a dark turn when a demon brutally killed his father, sending Lucas on a relentless quest for vengeance. With his childhood friend Aiko
yepplayerprofile.lucas3: \fs[23]\c[1]Kusuda by his side, they tracked the demon across the world of Cindrivia, forming bonds with trusted companions along the way.
yepplayerprofile.lucas4: \fs[23]\c[3]However, everything changed during the Goddess-rescue incident. Lucas uncovered Aiko’s true identity as the reincarnated Goddess of the Sun, and
yepplayerprofile.lucas5: \fs[23]\c[3]learned the deeper truth behind the demon's merciless actions. Realizing the greater purpose at stake, he abandoned his pursuit of revenge and chose
yepplayerprofile.lucas6: \fs[23]\c[3]to stand beside Aiko, aligning himself with her fight for justice and the will of the former Goddess. Together, they ventured to Valkadia, determined
yepplayerprofile.lucas7: \fs[23]\c[3]to stop the demon from destroying the Yggdrasil Tree—the core of the nine worlds.
yepplayerprofile.lucas8: \fs[23]\c[17]Once cold-hearted and consumed by vengeance, Lucas’s hardened exterior softened through his long journey with Aiko, her light thawing the darkness
yepplayerprofile.lucas9: \fs[23]\c[17]in his heart. Retiring from his life as a treasure hunter, he now embraces the title of 'Shadow Chaser', a name that strikes fear into the hearts
yepplayerprofile.lucas10: \fs[23]\c[17]of his enemies as he moves with the silence and swiftness of a shadow.

mapdesc.summerbreeze: A serene town where the air forever carries the scent of summer winds.
mapdesc.religia: A bustling desert-edge city, where trade flows as endlessly as the sands.
mapdesc.seagull: The lifeblood port of Valkadia, where all goods find passage across the seas.
mapdesc.haravi: A forest town steeped in calm, its air rich with the fragrance of leaves.
mapdesc.merinberg: A grand city seated high atop #{mapnames.merinmount}, kissed by the sky.
mapdesc.whitehaven: Once the proud home of the Griffinians, now a silent ruin of the past.
mapdesc.luvinia: A radiant city of faith, where voices rise in praise of Goddess Yurika.
mapdesc.valenka: Crowned by victory in the Four-Kingdom War, it stands as Valkadia’s capital.
mapdesc.shiroi: An elegant port of the east, where snow graces the land through every season.
mapdesc.serenus: A secluded island haven where elves live in quiet harmony, far from the mainland’s reach.

valenkamap.valenkamapdisable: The rowboats in #{mapnames.valenkacity} cannot be used right now.
valenkamap.valenkamapenable: The rowboats in #{mapnames.valenkacity} can be used again now.
valenkamap.introduction: There are several moorings around #{mapnames.valenkacity}. You can use the rowboats on moorings to move around the city faster. Please keep in mind that there will be some fees when using and the rowboats may not be available all the time.
valenkamap.boatfee: You need to pay \v[21] #{termwords.gold} to use the rowboat. Proceed?

generalskilldesc.tensionrelease: When your TP reaches 100, your next attack (or even healing magic) will deal bonus damage equal to (Your level / 20) per point of TP. If target is an enemy, also revealing one of its unknown weaknesses.
generalskilldesc.scan: Analyzes an enemy, exposing their stats and one of their unknown weaknesses. Has a 2-turn cooldown.

itemdesc.id0005: Revives a fainted ally with 25% HP recovered.
itemdesc.id0011: Halves the encounter rate against unseen monsters for 300 steps.
itemdesc.id0012: Totally prevents you from encountering unseen monsters for 200 steps.
itemdesc.id0016: Open this book to research skill remotely. It is lost when closed.
itemdesc.id0017: Use this scroll to gain access of crafting system remotely. It is lost upon usage.
itemdesc.id0019: Revives a fainted ally with full HP recovered.
itemdesc.id0021: A strong medicine mixed by The Eclipse Group. People smelling it is pushed into a certain illusion.
itemdesc.id0022: A book contains many tips and tricks to help the owner fight against nature.
itemdesc.id0023: An useful tool used for chopping and cutting things.
itemdesc.id0024: A small board made of wood.
itemdesc.id0025: A rope made of rock vines. Thin but tough.
itemdesc.id0026: A large board which can be used as a temporary bridge.
itemdesc.id0027: A magical crystal which can detect the source of the blight force inside #{placenames.coalmine}. Use it once you locate the source.
itemdesc.id0028: An useful tool used for breaking things.
itemdesc.id0029: An old rusty key. It has been here for a long time.
itemdesc.id0030: Lever of a switch. It should be useful in some certain cases.
itemdesc.id0033: A long rope which can be used as a detonator.
itemdesc.id0034: Hand-made dynamites. Enough explosive power to break solid things.
itemdesc.id0035: Only those who have this are able to set sail to the sea from #{mapnames.seagullport}.
itemdesc.id0036: The contract contains terms and conditions to join the Peacekeeper Squad.
itemdesc.id0037: A necklace powered by the Tsunami Sphere inside.
itemdesc.id0038: Embeded by the power of tsunami, this sphere is capable of extinguish even an eternal flame.
itemdesc.id0039: Embeded by the power of hellfire, this sphere is capable of enflaming everything.
itemdesc.id0040: Embeded by the power of living essence, this sphere is capable of bringing back life to every living thing.
itemdesc.id0041: Embeded by the power of calamity, this sphere is capable of taking life away.
itemdesc.id0042: A necklace powered by the Hellfire Sphere inside.
itemdesc.id0043: A necklace powered by the Spirit Sphere inside.
itemdesc.id0044: A necklace powered by the Doom Sphere inside.
itemdesc.id0045: Said to be the last gift of King Lawrence Heimdall for his people, this sphere is able to summon the Guardian Firebird of #{mapnames.merinberg}.
itemdesc.id0046: Legend told that this feather granted the user an ability to hover over the ground.
itemdesc.id0047: A small oil lamp which helps light the way. Use it here or press \c[2]1\c[0] to turn on/off.
itemdesc.id0048: Mysterious herb with strange shape and color.
itemdesc.id0049: A small shard with dim light. It is said to be formed by the unholy essence.
itemdesc.id0050: The letter from Father Zephaniah Whisperoak which introduces you to Mother Einhart Sylvian in #{mapnames.valenkacity}.
itemdesc.id0051: An official merchant card which allows you to enter #{mapnames.valenkacity} as an individual or a group of merchants.
itemdesc.id0052: A Holy Rood that allows the user to go deeply into their mind and discover their dark side. You can go to Einhart's room and step on the light to start the ritual.
itemdesc.id0053: A piece of bones from the unfortunate creatures sinked in the poisoning swamps.
itemdesc.id0054: Han's magical hammer. Who knows what he could do with this...
itemdesc.id0055: The mysterious cube containing a large amount of purging energy.
itemdesc.id0056: A golden necklace with a crimson ruby attached in the middle. It is Amelia Blackpearl's keepsake before the incident. The ruby looks loosened... Maybe...?
itemdesc.id0057: A golden necklace. It is Amelia Blackpearl's keepsake before the incident. The ruby has been taken out...
itemdesc.id0058: A rusty lever. Maybe it should fit into something.
itemdesc.id0059: A stone tablet with ancient incantation carved on it.
itemdesc.id0060: A small shard with bright light. It is said to be formed by the holy essence.
itemdesc.id0061: A small shard with crimson light. It is said to be formed by the living essence.
itemdesc.id0062: A small shard with verdant light. It is said to be formed by the chaos essence.
itemdesc.id0063a: A small vial containing cyan liquid. Maybe it has some special effects...?
itemdesc.id0063b: Remove 1 #{generalstate.ailment} state for the whole party.
itemdesc.id0064a: A small vial containing violet liquid. Maybe it has some special effects...?
itemdesc.id0065: A treasured artifact of Einhart, crafted by Professor Balthazar. It stands as a testament to her exceptional mastery of magic.
itemdesc.id0066: A document bearing Father Zephaniah’s rightful claim to his ship, serving as official proof of ownership.
itemdesc.id0501: Remove 1 #{generalstate.ailment} state for the whole party.
itemdesc.id0503: Grants an ally (excluding self) +50% CRI for 2 turns in exchange of 25% of current HP. Duration is reset when reapplying.
itemdesc.id0504: Drains 25% of an ally's current HP, but gives them back 25% of your current MP.
itemdesc.id0505: Drains 25% of an ally's current MP, but gives them back 25% of your current HP.
itemdesc.id0506: Increase #{termwords.fire} damage taken of a unit by 50% for 3 turns.  Duration is reset when reapplying.
itemdesc.id0507: Increase #{termwords.ice} damage taken of a unit by 50% for 3 turns.  Duration is reset when reapplying.
itemdesc.id0508: Increase #{termwords.thunder} damage taken of a unit by 50% for 3 turns.  Duration is reset when reapplying.
itemdesc.id0509: For a turn, the affected unit cannot get fainted. Taking damage from #{generalskilldesc.certain} skills removes the effect instantly. Cannot use on yourself.

of your HP as damage to a Boss.

Your healing skills have a 15% chance to recover your HP by 100% of your MAT if you are not 
the target.

Using skills has a 50% chance to amplify your healing effects by 5% (Maximum of 50%). 
If you use a healing spell, this effect will reset itself.

Normal attacks have a (10 + 10% of LUK)% chance to remove one #{generalstate.ailment}, 
then takes 3 turns to cooldown. Has no effect on enemies.
weapon.id0230: Normal attacks has a 50% chance to deal 100% of your DEF as bonus damage.

weapon.id0232: \c[6]Cracked Lightning\c[0] has a 25% chance to make target confused for a turn.
weapon.id0245: A successful critical hit has a 50% chance to reduce target's Action Gauge by 20%.
weapon.id0246: Bonus AGI is equal to 10% of user's STR. Maximum of 40 AGI.
weapon.id0247:
Normally attacking units affected by \c[6]Physical Weakness\c[0] & \c[6]Armor Breaker\c[0] now deal bonus 
1.5x MAT + 0.5x LUK as #{termwords.darkness} damage.
weapon.id0248: For each \c[3]Positive\c[0] state affected, target receives more 10% of healing effects.
weapon.id0249:
Provides a 35% chance to add a random portion between 1% and 25% of your current HP to your direct 
damage dealt.
weapon.id0250:
If target successfully evades your physical attacks, it still takes 50% of your DEF as damage. 
If you miss your own physical attacks, your target only takes 25% of your DEF as damage.
weapon.id0251:
When you start casting a spell, you gain +10% magical damage dealt and +50% CRIT DMG until 
the end of your current action.
weapon.id0252:
Provides MDF bonus equal to 25% of your #{termwords.int} and amplifies \c[3]Arctic Tsunami\c[0] damage dealt 
by 25% of your MDF per hit.
weapon.id0253: \c[3]Sun Blaster\c[0] deals double damage to #{termwords.undead} and #{termwords.demon} monsters.
weapon.id0254: \c[3]Moon Strike\c[0] has a 15% chance to stun an #{termwords.undead} or #{termwords.demon} target for a turn.
weapon.id0255: A successful critical hit has a 30% chance to reduce target's DEF by 20% for 2 turns.
weapon.id0256: Allows \c[3]Poison Dart\c[0] to deal bonus initial damage equal to 50% of your LUK.
weapon.id0293: Increases ATK by 4, while decreases AGI by 2 per turn, up to 30 turns.
weapon.id0294: For every 50 missing MP, the damage dealt from skills increases by 10%, up to 100%.
weapon.id0295: Deals extra damage to the target equal to 25% of LUK per #{generalstate.ailment} state the target possesses.
weapon.id0296:
When healing an ally via direct healing effect, you gain 5% of their HP recovery per #{generalstate.positive} state 
they possess.
weapon.id0297:
Has a 50% chance to deal extra instance of damage equal to 50% of original damage plus 100% of MDF 
when dealing direct physical or certain damage.
weapon.id0299:
Critical magical & certain damage breaks target's MDF by 5 per instance for 7 turns. Turns are reset 
when reapplying this state.
weapon.id0300:
Normal attacks deal damage randomly between 10% to 300% of MAT and if hit, has a 25% chance to 
cast a random learned magic to a random target without spending MP.
weapon.id0301:
Dealing direct damage to an enemy has a 30% chance to turn it into \c[4]#{termwords.demon}\c[0] type for 5 turns. Turns are 
reset when reapplying this state.
weapon.id0302:
When your spell targets an ally, they have a 50% chance to receive a buff which raises their Action Gauge by 5% 
per instance when their turn ends for 5 turns, up to 10 instances. Reapplying this state also has a 35% chance to reset turns.
weapon.id0303:
Damaging the poisoned/bled enemies has a 35% chance to increase LUK by 15 per instance for 4 turns, 
up to 20 instances. Turns are reset when reapplying this state.
weapon.id0304:
Critical hits have a 50% chance to reduce target's AGI by 10 per turn, up to 100 AGI reduction. Reapplying 
this state has a 35% chance to reset turns.
weapon.id0317: Has a \c[4]50 + [LUK / 10]%\c[0] (Maximum of 75%) chance to deal bonus damage equal to LUK.
weapon.id0318: Allows \c[3]Sky Smasher\c[0] to be used as counter-attack with +50% damage.
weapon.id0319: Stores 10% of direct healing amount when you heal allies via your skills and adds it to your next \c[4]#{termwords.magic}\c[0] as bonus damage. Maximum of 500 stored damage.
weapon.id0320: Targets of your skills gain 5 bonus AGI if they are your allies (excluding you), or lose 5 AGI if they are your enemies for 3 turns. Stackable at a maximum of +/- 50 AGI. Reapplying resets turn.
weapon.id0321: Your skills gain +50% CRI when damaging \c[4]Taunted\c[0] enemies.
weapon.id0322: Damage dealt increases by 5% per 20% of Aggro. Starts from 100% of Aggro. Maximum of 75% bonus damage.
weapon.id0323: Double all your \c[17]#{termwords.thunder}\c[0] and \c[18]#{termwords.fire}\c[0] damage dealt.
weapon.id0324: \c[9]#{termwords.water}\c[0] and \c[23]#{termwords.ice}\c[0] damage have a 50% chance to cause \c[4]Corrosion\c[0] to the enemies, dealing 1% of their max HP per turn for 5 turns. Cannot reapply.
weapon.id0325: Damaging enemies has a 50% chance to heal the ally with the lowest HP equal to 30% of the damage dealt.
weapon.id0326: Using \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0] provides you 1 \c[4]Choir\c[0] stack for 5 turns. Each increases your \c[6]#{termwords.holy}\c[0] damage by 10%. Maximum of 20 stacks.
weapon.id0327: Adds 50% of your ATK to your skills as bonus damage.

armor.id0316: Taking physical damage provides you a 15% chance to increase your Action Gauge by 15%.
armor.id0317: Taking physical damage provides you a 15% chance to decrease damage taken by 10%.
armor.id0341: Taking magical damage provides you a 25% chance to increase your Action Gauge by 10%.
armor.id0346:
Taking magical damage provides you a 20% chance to recover your HP by 50% of your MAT
 per turn for 3 turns.
armor.id0371: Provides +10% chance to block magical damage.
armor.id0373:
Taking magical damage provides you a 25% chance to nullify and reflect half of the damage taken 
to the owner.
armor.id0374:
Reduces #{termwords.fire} damage taken by 50%. Taking physical damage provides you a 40% chance to deal 
100% of your MDF as #{termwords.fire} damage to the owner.
armor.id0380:
Taking magical damage provides you a 10% chance to receive a small amount of #{termwords.gold} 
equal to 10% of damage taken. Maximum of 1000 #{termwords.gold}.
armor.id0381: At the end of your turn, you have a 20% chance to automatically remove a #{generalstate.sleep} state.
armor.id0397: Decreases magical damage taken by 25% of your MDF.
armor.id0399: All your successful counter-attacks have +25% damage dealt.
armor.id0400: Taking damage during \c[4]#{battletext.guard}\c[0] has a 25% chance to decrease attacker's ATK by 25% for a turn.
armor.id0401: Using magical damage skills has a 35% chance to return 50% of the MP cost.
armor.id0402: Provides a 50% chance to reduce magical damage taken by 50% of your LUK.
armor.id0404: Getting hit by critical damage raises your Action Gauge by 20%.
armor.id0405: Increases direct MP recovery received from items by 20%.
armor.id0406:
Provides a [LUK * 0.2]% chance to gain 2x \c[4]Bronze Ingots\c[0] or 2x \c[4]Copper Ingots\c[0] when damaging 
enemies. Maximum of 10 Ingots per battle.
armor.id0407:
\fs[23]A critical hit has a 50% chance to grant all your party (reserved members only receive 75%) bonus EXP equal to 
40% of the damage dealt. Distributed evenly among all members, excluding fainted ones. Maximum of 3000 EXP per battle.
armor.id0408: \fs[25]Dealing magical damage to the enemies provides a 20% chance to earn bonus TP equal to 20% of your MAT.
armor.id0409: Returns 20% of damage taken to the attacker upon a successful physical block.
armor.id0414: Reduces damage taken from counter-attacks by 20%.
armor.id0425: Each point of #{termwords.vit} increases LUK by 1. Maximum of 50 LUK.
armor.id0426:
Each critical hit you take before your turn heals you by 50 HP when your turn starts. Maximum of 10 
critical hits.
armor.id0427: Each point of #{termwords.spr} increases MHP by 3. Maximum of 500 MHP
armor.id0428:
Reduces damage taken from counter-attacks by 35%. Starting battle with a barrier which blocks incoming 
damage equal to 15% of MHP for 10 turns. Maximum of 1000 barrier points.
armor.id0430:
Each point of #{termwords.vit} increases MMP by 5 and each 2 points of #{termwords.dex} increases DEF by 1. Maximum of 
500 MMP and 100 DEF.
armor.id0431: Each point of INT increases MHP by 1. Maximum of 250 MHP.
armor.id0432:
Receiving direct healing effects from skills has a 50% chance to reduce non-certain damage taken by 
50% for one hit, or after one turn.
armor.id0433: Gains +10 DEF for every \c[2]#{generalstate.ailment}\c[0] state, and +10 MDF for every \c[3]#{generalstate.positive}\c[0] state you possess.
armor.id0434: Increases all direct damage dealt by 15%.
armor.id0435: Ending turn always gives you a 15% of Action Gauge pre-filled.
armor.id0436:
If your attacks miss the target, it still has a 50% chance to take damage equal to 100% of your 
highest stat between ATK, MAT, AGI, or LUK as non-direct damage.
armor.id0437: Provides a 20% chance to block incoming magical damage.

bossweapon.id0708: Grants \c[6]+1 LUK\c[0] for every \c[6]2.5 INT\c[0] and \c[6]+1 MDF\c[0] for every \c[6]2 SPR\c[0].

bossarmor.id0701: MDF points of this armor are equal to 15% of user's VIT points. Maximum of 30 MDF.
bossarmor.id0702: When user takes critical damage, there is a 50% chance to gain +25% EVA or MEV based on physical or magical damage he takes.
bossarmor.id0703: When user takes magical damage, there is a 20% chance that the attacker takes 25% of user's MAT as damage.
bossarmor.id0705: Reduces damage taken from magical attacks by 20%. Physical attackers have a 50% chance to take 30% of damage dealt as reflected damage.

Decreases Aggro by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1123:
Increases BLKV by 25 (#{crystalsdesc.unstackable}).
Increases CNT by 10% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1124:
Increases EXR by 4%.
Increases TCR by 9%.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.stackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1125:
\fs[24]Refunds 6% of spent MP per affected allies when casting direct healing skills (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Amplifies all direct healing effects by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1126:
Regenerates 7 MP and 30 HP per #{generalstate.ailment} state per turn.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1127:
\fs[21]Increases 20 points of a specific stat based on equipped weapon types: \c[4]#{termwords.dagger}\c[0] - LUK, \c[4]#{termwords.sword}\c[0] - ATK, \c[4]#{termwords.whip} & #{termwords.staff}\c[0] - MAT, \c[4]#{termwords.gauntlet}\c[0] - DEF, \c[4]#{termwords.magictome}\c[0] - MDF. Increases CRI by 15% and CRIT DMG by 75%.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.stackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1128:
\fs[22]All direct damage dealt increases by 20%, but you also take half of that portion as damage (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Provides +12% Physical Lifesteal (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}). Converts 4% of damage dealt into MP (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1129:
Provides a bonus of 90 flat damage to critical hits. Increases CRIT DMG by 75%. Increases MAT by 10%.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.stackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1130:
\fs[23]Negates one physical or magical hit every 3 turns (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
Decreases Aggro by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}). Increases MHP by 10%.(#{crystalsdesc.stackable}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1131:
\fs[21]Increases EVA & MEV by 13% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}). Suffering critical physical/magical damage has a 45% chance to increase your damage reduction by 10%. Maximum of 60%. Reset at the end of your turn when reach its maximum value (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1132:
Increases BLKV by 25 and provides 30% chance to block magical damage (#{crystalsdesc.unstackable}).
Increases CNT by 10% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1133:
Increases EXR by 4% and TCR by 9%.
Increases #{termwords.gold} received from battle by 20%.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.stackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1134:
\fs[23]Reduce MCR by 7% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}). Refunds 8% of spent MP per affected allies when casting direct healing skills (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Amplifies all direct healing effects by 20% (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1135:
Regenerates 10 MP and 50 HP per #{generalstate.ailment} state per turn (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). For every 100 damage taken, your HP is recovered by 40 at the start of your turn (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1136:
\fs[20]Increases ATK & MAT by 100, but decreases MHP & MMP by 25%. Damaging normal enemies have a 25% chance to deal \c[4]9999\c[0] damage. If the target is a Boss, the bonus damage is \c[4]1000\c[0], and you take a quarter to yourself (Can't kill yourself). Works once per turn and 5 times per battle. \c[4]Detaching destroys the equipped equipment\c[0].
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1137:
\fs[22]Increases DEF & MDF by 100, but decreases ATK & MAT by 25%. Once per turn, heals an ally with the lowest HP Rate (except yourself) by \c[4]100% of your (DEF + MDF)\c[0] at the end of your turn when their HP drops below 50%. Works 5 times per battle. \c[4]Detaching destroys the equipped equipment\c[0].
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1139:
Increases MHP by 1000 and MMP by 500, but decreases AGI & LUK by 25%. Once per turn, revives one fainted ally and fully recovers their HP at the start of your turn. Works twice per battle. \c[4]Detaching destroys the equipped equipment\c[0].
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1141:
Using #{termwords.magic} provides you +3 MAT until reaching 30 bonus MAT, or by the end of the battle.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1142:
Stores 5% of all damage taken before your turn and adds it to your next attack. Resets when the damage is dealt or by the end of your turn.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1143:
Taking physical damage provides you +1 DEF until reaching 30 bonus DEF, or by the end of the battle.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1144:
Reduces physical damage taken by 5% per alive enemy. The amount is double for a Boss. Maximum of 20% damage reduction.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1145:
Decreases damage taken from #{termwords.slash} and #{termwords.pierce} by 10%.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.stackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1146:
Using #{termwords.aid} skills provides a 50% chance to recover your HP equal to 150% of the spent MP by the end of your action.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1147:
At the start of your turn, if your MP drops below 25%, generates 4 MP per turn for 5 turns. The effect does not trigger if you are already affected.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1148:
Evading an attack provides you +5 LUK until reaching 25 bonus LUK, or by the end of the battle.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}, #{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}.
crystalsdesc.id1149:
Embeds #{termwords.thunder} element to your attacks. For every 18 DEF of the target, your physical damage dealt increases by 2%, with a maximum value of 125% (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1150:
Using #{termwords.magic} provides you +5 MAT until reaching 50 bonus MAT, or by the end of the battle (#{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}). Guarantees a critical hit on every 7th magical hit (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1151:
\fs[23]Stores 10% of all damage taken before your turn and adds it to your next attack. Resets when the damage is dealt or by the end of your turn (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). When your HP is lower than 65%, increases your ATK by 30% but decreases your REC by 20% (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1152:
Taking physical damage provides you +2 DEF until reaching 50 bonus DEF, or by the end of the battle (#{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}). Decreases direct physical damage taken by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1153: \fs[21]Reduces physical damage taken by 8% per alive enemy. The amount is double for a Boss. Maximum of 30% damage reduction (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). crystalsdesc.id1159:
Using #{termwords.magic} provides you +7 MAT until reaching 70 bonus MAT, or by the end of the battle (#{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}). Guarantees a critical hit on every 5th magical hit (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Increases your MAT by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable})
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.

Charges reset when reaching the maximum amount of 15 charges at the start of your turn (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). #{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.offensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.

\fs[23]Taking physical damage provides you +3 DEF until reaching 70 bonus DEF, or by the end of the battle (#{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}). Decreases direct physical damage taken by 20% (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Increases your CNT by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.

Chance increases by 5% for every 10% of missing HP (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Increases your DEF by 12% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}). #{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1163:
Decreases damage taken from #{termwords.slash} and #{termwords.pierce} by 16%, increases BLKV by 40 and GRD by 35%.
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.defensive} - #{crystalsdesc.stackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1164:
\fs[22]Using #{termwords.aid} skills provides a 50% chance to recover your HP equal to 300% of the spent MP by the end of your action (#{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}). #{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1165: \fs[22]At the start of your turn, if your MP drops below 35%, generates 6 MP per turn for 5 turns. Provides a 35% chance to recover MP equal to 5% of the received healing amount from healing skills (#{crystalsdesc.highestrarity}). Increases REC by 15% (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}). #{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.
crystalsdesc.id1166:
Evading an attack provides you +15 LUK until reaching 75 bonus LUK, or by the end of the battle (#{crystalsdesc.differentrarity}). Increases LUK by 9%, and ATK, MAT, DEF, MDF by 15 (#{crystalsdesc.stackable}).
#{crystalsdesc.type}: #{crystalsdesc.supporting} - #{crystalsdesc.unstackable}.

shadowwalkerskill.coupdegrace: \fs[24]Strikes with lethal precision multiple times, dealing \c[4]100% of (AGI + LUK)\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<$gameActors.actor(6).agi+$gameActors.actor(6).luk>>\c[0]) as \c[4]Certain\c[0] damage (#{generalskilldesc.slash} or #{generalskilldesc.pierce} per hit). Applies \c[4]Disabled\c[0], draining \c[4]1% Max HP\c[0] per turn with a \c[4]20%\c[0] chance to skip a turn for \c[4]3\c[0] turns. Maximizes \c[4]Deadly Poison\c[0] or \c[2]Bleeding\c[0] from \c[4]Assassination\c[0] damage pools if active.
shadowwalkerskill.shadowspec: \fs[25]Haunts all enemies, reducing \c[4]HIT by 50%\c[0] for \c[4]3\c[0] turns or \c[4]2\c[0] missed attacks. Missed physical attacks trigger \c[4][100 + Skill Level x 5]% of ATK\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<Math.ceil((1+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(951)*0.05)*$gameActors.actor(6).atk)>>\c[0]) + \c[4][50 + Skill Level x 5]% of AGI\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<Math.ceil((0.5+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(951)*0.05)*$gameActors.actor(6).agi)>>\c[0]) damage and a \c[4][Skill Level + 10]%\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<10+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(951)>>%\c[0]) chance to stun for 1 turn, ending the effect. \c[4]4-turn cooldown\c[0].
shadowwalkerskill.shadowillu: Every \c[4][10 - Skill Level / 4]\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<10-Math.round($gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(952)/4)>>\c[0]) non-certain attacks received, boosts \c[4]EVA by 100%\c[0] and \c[4]MEV by 50%\c[0]. Lost after evasion or on turn end with cooldown. Grants \c[4]01 Mastery Points\c[0] (30% chance) on damage, or \c[4]03 Mastery Points\c[0] on trigger.
shadowwalkerskill.venomtouch: \fs[20]Inflicts stacking venom for \c[4]4\c[0] turns. Physical damage has a \c[4][20 + Skill Level x 2]%\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<20+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(953)*2>>%\c[0]) chance to poison, dealing \c[4][30 + Skill Level x 2]% of LUK\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<Math.ceil((0.3+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(953)*0.02)*$gameActors.actor(6).luk)>>\c[0]) per turn. At max stacks or target death, explodes, dealing max poison damage to nearby allies and transferring venom. Reapplying adds \c[4]2\c[0] stacks and refreshes duration. \c[4]4-turn cooldown\c[0].
shadowwalkerskill.fatalblows: \fs[20]After evading \c[4][5 - Skill Level / 7]\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<5-Math.round($gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(954)/7)>>\c[0]) non-certain attacks, your next \c[4][1 + Skill Level / 5]\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<1+Math.round($gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(954)/5)>>\c[0]) attacks gain \c[4]+150% CRIT\c[0] and deal \c[4]1% of target’s current HP\c[0] as bonus damage. Lasts 1 turn, disabled until all bonus attacks are used, then cools down for 1 turn. Grants \c[4]01 Mastery Points\c[0] (30% chance) on damage, or \c[4]03 Mastery Points\c[0] on evasion threshold.
shadowwalkerskill.shadowdance: \fs[18]Performs a shadowy dance, slashing an enemy \c[4]11\c[0] times, each dealing \c[4][35 + Skill Level x 2]% of ATK\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<Math.ceil((0.35+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(955)*0.02)*$gameActors.actor(6).atk)>>\c[0]), \c[4][30 + Skill Level x 2]% of LUK\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<Math.ceil((0.3+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(955)*0.02)*$gameActors.actor(6).luk)>>\c[0]), or \c[4][40 + Skill Level x 2]% of AGI\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<Math.ceil((0.4+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(955)*0.02)*$gameActors.actor(6).agi)>>\c[0]) as certain slash damage. Under \c[4]Shadow Camouflage\c[0], deals damage with \c[4]+100% CRIT & +40% CRIT DMG\c[0] and has a \c[4][25 + Skill Level x 5]%\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<25+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(955)*5>>%\c[0]) chance per hit to reduce non-certain damage output by \c[4][3 + Skill Level]%\c[0] (\c[2]\evalText<<3+$gameActors.actor(6).skillMasteryLevel(955)>>%\c[0]) for \c[4]3\c[0] turns (stackable). Removes \c[4]Shadow Camouflage\c[0] and exhausts you for 1 turn.

After 3 turns, deals \c[4][(1.25x MAT) + (Target's Chrono Stacks x MAT)]\c[0] plus \c[4]25%\c[0] of direct damage taken during the effect (max \c[4]500\c[0] bonus damage). Recasting on the same target adds \c[4]1 Chrono Stack\c[0].

Recasting on the same target resets duration and adds \c[4]1 Nether Flame stack\c[0] (max \c[4]5\c[0]).
luciaskill.backtrack: Grants a \c[4]25%\c[0] chance to nullify \c[4]3\c[0] physical or magical damage sources per turn.
luciaskill.prevision: Peers into the future, granting \c[4]+20% CNT\c[0] and enabling \c[4]Chrono Blaster\c[0] as a counter to physical or magical attacks.

itemskill.id0298: Decreases all damage dealt by 25%, but provides +50% All Lifesteal for 3 turns.
itemskill.id0851: Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that increases ATK by 50 but decreases DEF by 75 for a turn. Has a 1-turn cooldown.
itemskill.id0852: Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that increases CRIT DMG by 100% for a turn. Has a 1-turn cooldown.
itemskill.id0853:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a debuff on enemy for 2 turns, increase their damage taken from 
#{termwords.slash}, #{termwords.pierce} and #{termwords.blunt} by 25%.
itemskill.id0854:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that provides a buff for 4 turns, providing you a 50% chance to heal all your allies 
based on 50% of the damage dealt, and distributed evenly among all allies (excluding you).
itemskill.id0855:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that provides a buff for 5 turns, each time you damage your opponent, the target has 
a 50% chance to get their DEF reduced by 5. Stack infinitely. The DEF reduction effect lasts 3 turns.
itemskill.id0856:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that provides a buff for 5 turns, each time you damage your opponent, your own DEF 
increases by 5. Maximum of 10 Instances. Cannot reapply.
itemskill.id0857:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a debuff on enemy for 3 turns, increase their damage taken from #{termwords.fire}, 
#{termwords.thunder} and #{termwords.earth} by 50%.
itemskill.id0858:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a debuff on enemy for 3 turns, increase their damage taken from #{termwords.ice}, 
#{termwords.water} and #{termwords.holy} by 50%.
itemskill.id0859:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a buff to an ally for 5 turns, each time they deal damage, their HP will be 
recovered based on 25% of your MDF. Cannot reapply. Has a 3-turn cooldown.
itemskill.id0860:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a buff to an ally for 5 turns, boosting their CRI by 25% and CRIT DMG by 
50%. Cannot reapply. Has a 3-turn cooldown.
itemskill.id0861:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a buff for 3 turns, providing you a 50% chance to deal double damage on 
targets affected by #{generalstate.poison} or #{generalstate.bleed} states. Cannot reapply.
itemskill.id0862:
Grants an #{termwords.aid} skill that adds a debuff on enemy for 3 turns, allowing your allies to deal extra 
25% of physical damage to it (excluding you). Cannot reapply.

skillresetmaster.npc3: Hmm... To reset your skills, you will be charged \c[6]\v[898]\c[0] #{termwords.gold}. Are you sure you want to do it?

skillresetmaster.npc5: I'm sorry but you do not have enough #{termwords.gold}.

statresetmaster.npc1: I sense a restless power within you, teetering on the edge of balance. If you seek guidance, I can help you steady its course and regain control.
statresetmaster.npc2: Speak the name, and I shall act.
statresetmaster.npc3: Your body harbors \c[4]\v[914]\c[0] unstable sources of power (JP). To bring balance, you must offer \c[6]\v[913]\c[0] #{termwords.gold} as tribute, stabilizing your power. Do you accept?
statresetmaster.npc4:
It is done! May you wield your power with greater wisdom in the future.
Would you now seek to bring balance to others as well?

mainQobjective.description: The full record about the incidents happening in the World of Valkadia.
mainQobjective.reward: A full understanding about the story of the game.
mainQobjective.step2: Make your way out of the camp.
mainQobjective.step4: Find the hidden entrance of The Eclipse Group in Morrigan's House.
mainQobjective.step6: Enter The Eclipse HQ to find \n[2].
mainQobjective.step10: Talk to two shopkeepers in the #{placenames.magicshop}.
mainQobjective.step12: Find out who is crying in #{mapnames.sunlightriver}.
mainQobjective.step22: Make your way through the culvert.
mainQobjective.step23: Find a way to reach the door on the other side.
mainQobjective.step27: Get back to The Eclipse HQ in #{mapnames.summerbreeze}.
mainQobjective.step28: Talk to Harley in The Eclipse HQ.
mainQobjective.step34: Talk to the Boss of #{placenames.antiservant} on the second floor.
mainQobjective.step38: Talk to #{npcnames.father} Albert in the library.
mainQobjective.step44: Get back to The Eclipse HQ.
mainQobjective.step47: Locate your allied cargo ships somewhere in #{mapnames.sanguineocean}.
mainQobjective.step50: Destroy the shining crystal on the Relic.
mainQobjective.step51: Make your way to #{mapnames.sandstorm} (By going South from #{mapnames.religiacity}).
mainQobjective.step54: Make your way to Merinberg City Hall.
mainQobjective.step56: Bring the Peacekeeper Squad Contract to General Helena Winston at the #{placenames.militaryhq}.
mainQobjective.step57: Speak with the #{npcnames.recep} in the #{placenames.militaryhq} to take your contract back.
mainQobjective.step58: Speak with General Helena Winston in her office.
mainQobjective.step60: Locate the Tomb of King Lawrence Heimdall.
mainQobjective.step61: Enter the Tomb of King Lawrence Heimdall.
mainQobjective.step62: Find a way to approach the locked gate inside the tomb.
mainQobjective.step64: Take the stairs to reach the #{placenames.merinpeak} and find a way to summon Guardian Firebird.
mainQobjective.step65: Get back to Helena's office and talk to her.
mainQobjective.step67: Find Lawrence Heimdall's office on the second floor of Merinberg City Hall.
mainQobjective.step69: Find out what's happened at Merinberg #{placenames.church}.
mainQobjective.step73: Find a way to activate the sealed gate inside #{placenames.fabledgateway}.
mainQobjective.step74: Put the rainbow feather into the strange machine.
mainQobjective.step75: Make use of the light bridge to cross the quicksand area.
mainQobjective.step76: Light all four torches up to open the portal to #{mapnames.whitehaven}.
mainQobjective.step79: Scout #{mapnames.whitehaven} to look for some survivors.
mainQobjective.step81: Get back to The Eclipse HQ in #{mapnames.summerbreeze}.
mainQobjective.step83: Enter the #{lorewords.relicofvendurousleaf} beneath The Eclipse HQ.
mainQobjective.step87: Find the office of Great Bishop Zephaniah in the #{placenames.conferencehall}.
mainQobjective.step88: Collect 06 Spirit Herbs at the #{placenames.spiritwell} and bring back to Father Zephaniah.
mainQobjective.step93: Locate the captive merchant inside the #{placenames.abandonedmine}.
mainQobjective.step96: Get through the north brige of #{mapnames.valenkacity} to find the Cathedral.
mainQobjective.step97: Locate and enter the Cathedral inside #{placenames.valenkaroyalpalace}.
mainQobjective.step98: Look for Mother Einhart Sylvian on the second floor of the Cathedral.
mainQobjective.step99: Explore #{placenames.labyrinth} to find the way out.
mainQobjective.step101: Gather \c[4]09\c[0] \c[3]Withered Bones\c[0] from the dead bodies of small animals in #{mapnames.chaoticswamp} (\evalText<<$gameParty.numItems($dataItems[53])>>/9).
mainQobjective.step102: Find \n[14] somewhere around #{mapnames.valenkacity} (Try to talk with people with \c[17]!\c[0] on their heads).
mainQobjective.step104: Bring the \ii[54] back to Han in #{mapnames.valenkacity}.
mainQobjective.step105: Inform Mother Einhart about the time to forge the \ii[55] in her office at #{placenames.valenkaroyalpalace}.
mainQobjective.step106: Enter the Royal Castle at #{placenames.valenkaroyalpalace} to meet King Cedric Wisebrook.
mainQobjective.step107: Collect the \ii[55] from Han the Blacksmith.
mainQobjective.step108: Bring the \ii[55] to Mother Einhart at her office.
mainQobjective.step109: Get back to #{placenames.valenkamainhall} inside the Royal Castle.
mainQobjective.step110: Find Lady Amelia's office on #{placenames.valenkamainhall2nd} inside the Royal Castle.
mainQobjective.step111: Get inside the #{lorewords.infinitychamber} through the portal in Lady Amelia's office.
mainQobjective.step113: Head to #{mapnames.seishinwood} by going east of #{mapnames.luviniacity}.
mainQobjective.step116: Go to the #{placenames.shiroitradinghall} in the northern part of the town to find Ryland and Plati.
mainQobjective.step117: Look for Plati somewhere around south of #{mapnames.shiroitown}.
mainQobjective.step118: Return to the #{placenames.shiroitradinghall} in #{mapnames.shiroitown} to meet Ryland upstairs.
mainQobjective.step119: Take Plati back to Mother Einhart in #{mapnames.valenkacity}.
mainQobjective.step122: Find #{npcnames.alchemist} #{npcnames.valenkaalchemist}'s house located northwest part of Valenka's inner city.
mainQobjective.step124: Find the house of #{npcnames.valenkaalchemist}'s partner next to the #{placenames.shiroitradinghall}.
mainQobjective.step125: Get down to the secret basement inside Kaoru's house.
mainQobjective.step126: Return to #{mapnames.valenkacity} to meet Mother Einhart at her office.
mainQobjective.step127: Get to #{mapnames.voicelessjungle} to find Einhart and \n[11].
mainQobjective.step128: Talk to Einhart at Father Zephaniah's Tower in #{mapnames.luviniacity}.
mainQobjective.step131: Find the town of the elves somewhere in #{mapnames.verdantfield}.
mainQobjective.step132: Use the \c[4]\ii[65]\c[0] to solve the mysterious riddle from the elf in #{mapnames.verdantfield}. (You can step over the yellow \c[17]!\c[0] and use the tool).
mainQobjective.step133: Walk through the bridge in #{mapnames.verdantfield} to reach #{mapnames.serenetown}.
mainQobjective.step134: Find Professor Balthazar in his academy located north of the #{mapnames.serenetown}.
mainQobjective.step135: Find Professor Balthazar's office in his academy.
mainQobjective.step136: Leave #{mapnames.serenetown} to the west to find #{placenames.niveuslab}.

awakeningquests.description: Awaken the hidden potential deep inside one's mind.

aiko.step1: Enter the magic circle in Einhart's office to challenge the absolute side of \n[5]'s soul.

lucas.step1: Enter the magic circle in Einhart's office to challenge the dark side of \n[6]'s soul.

brokencrate1.interact1: A broken crate. There should be something to do with it...

vine1.interact1: A long vine is crawling on the wall...?

bridge1.interact1: Better find something to connect two sides together.

boulder1.interact1: This boulder is blocking the way. Maybe something can break it?

firstbattle.mes1: Ah, perfect timing! We've run into some trouble, and I could use a hand. But first, let me walk you through a few things about \c[4]Battle Mode\c[0]. You are not familiar with this, are you?
firstbattle.messkip: Looks like we've got an experienced warrior right here. Alright! I will leave the rest to you!
firstbattle.messkipno: Okay! Pay attention then! I'm about to start now!
firstbattle.mes2: You can see me here, right? This is where I'll be standing during the fight.
firstbattle.mes3: And over there! Those are our enemies. Poor souls picked the wrong fight.
firstbattle.mes4: Now, look up here. This shows the turn order. We’ll need to wait our turn before making a move, so stay sharp.
firstbattle.mes5: See the \c[17]Action Gauge\c[0] by my portrait? I have to wait for it to fill up before I can act. Same goes for them too, thankfully.
firstbattle.mes6: How fast the gauge fills depends on \c[11]#{termwords.agi} (AGI)\c[0]. The higher it is, the faster we get to act.
firstbattle.mes7: Now down here! This shows my important stats: \c[2]Hit Points (HP)\c[0], \c[1]Mana Points (MP)\c[0], \c[3]Tension Points (TP)\c[0], and any \c[5]Status Effects\c[0] I might have.
firstbattle.mes8: And here’s the good part! The \c[4]Battle Commands\c[0]! Every move I can make is listed here.
firstbattle.mes9: Alright, enough talking! Let's get started. Pick the \c[4]#{battletext.attack}\c[0] command so I can land a hit!
firstbattle.mes10: Good hit! But it’s not quite enough to bring them down.
firstbattle.mes10b: I've got a little trick up my sleeve. Choose the \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0] command and pick \c[4]True Strike\c[0]!
firstbattle.mes11: Skills under \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0] are real lifesavers. Keep them in mind!
firstbattle.mes12: Now that \c[4]True Strike\c[0] is up, I’m feeling stronger! Let’s follow up with an \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0]. Pick the \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0] command and use \c[4]Sky Smasher\c[0]!
firstbattle.mes13: See that? Using \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0] skills together with \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0] makes for a powerful combo!
firstbattle.mes13b: Shit! Their turn’s coming! I need to brace for impact! Help me pick the \c[4]#{battletext.guard}\c[0] command!
firstbattle.mes14: Ouch! That hurt! We’ll need to be careful or we’re done for!
firstbattle.mes14b: Time to patch up. I’ve got a spare \c[4]\ii[1]\c[0] in my bag. Use the \c[4]#{battletext.item}\c[0] command and pick \c[4]\ii[1]\c[0] to heal me up!
firstbattle.mes15: That’s better! Now, let’s show them our \c[4]#{termwords.ultimate}\c[0] power! Pick the \c[4]#{termwords.ultimate}\c[0] command and use \c[4]Chaos Extermination\c[0]!
firstbattle.mes16: Victory is ours! You are truly a natural talent! From here on out, the battlefield is yours to command. Good luck!
firstbattle.mes17: \c[4]True Strike\c[0] and \c[4]Chaos Extermination\c[0] will be removed from \n[1]'s skill list for now, but don’t worry — they can be learned again later!

battlecommands.title: Battle Commands
battlecommands.description1: During battle, you can access several sub battle commands to better manage your party. Press \c[4]ESC\c[0], \c[4]X\c[0], or \c[4]Right Click\c[0] before selecting an action to open the sub battle command menu. Each command is explained on the next page.
battlecommands.page2description1: - \c[4]#{battletext.fight}\c[0]: Continue the current battle as usual
battlecommands.page2description2: - \c[4]#{termwords.autobattle}\c[0]: Let the AI take control of your party and fight automatically
battlecommands.page2description3: - \c[4]#{yepinbattlestatus.commandtext}\c[0]: View detailed information about your characters, including any active \c[5]Status Effects\c[0]
battlecommands.page2description4: - \c[4]#{yepinbattlestatus.enemycommandtext}\c[0]: View detailed information about enemies, including any active \c[5]Status Effects\c[0]
battlecommands.page2description5: - \c[4]#{battletext.escape}\c[0]: Attempt to flee from the current battle. The success rate depends on the difference between your party’s total \c[11]#{termwords.agi} (AGI)\c[0] and that of the enemies
battlecommands.page3description1: Remember to check these commands often, especially the \c[4]#{yepinbattlestatus.commandtext}\c[0] and \c[4]#{yepinbattlestatus.enemycommandtext}\c[0]. Knowing our foes is half the battle won!
battlecommands.page3description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

statsallocation.title: #{menu.statinvestment}
statsallocation.description1: Each time a character levels up, they gain \c[4]06 JPs\c[0] (Job Points). These points are used to enhance your \c[4]Main Stats\c[0], which directly influence all your \c[4]Basic Parameters\c[0]. This is one of the primary ways to strengthen your characters.
statsallocation.description2: There are five \c[4]Main Stats\c[0]: \c[17]Vitality\c[0], \c[2]Strength\c[0], \c[3]Dexterity\c[0], \c[9]Intelligence\c[0], and \c[27]Spirit\c[0]. To invest your JP into these \c[4]Main Stats\c[0], open your \c[4]Menu\c[0], then select \c[4]#{menu.statinvestment}\c[0]. In this menu, you can view your \c[3]Available JPs\c[0], \c[2]Spent JPs\c[0], the values and details of all \c[4]Main Stats\c[0], and the corresponding \c[4]Basic Parameters\c[0].
statsallocation.page2description1: You can track changes in \c[4]Basic Parameters\c[0] in real-time as you assign JPs to the \c[4]Main Stats\c[0]. This allows you to determine which stats need investment for different characters.
statsallocation.page3description1: Once you've invested JPs, the changes cannot be undone. However, you can reset the invested stats and reclaim your JPs to reassign them from the beginning. To do this, visit the \c[4]#{npcnames.statmaster}\c[0] in the \c[4]#{placenames.magicshop}s\c[0] or \c[4]#{placenames.church}es\c[0] located in major cities. Each reset costs \c[3]50\c[0] per \c[2]Spent JP\c[0] (\c[6]#{termwords.gold}\c[0]).
statsallocation.page3description2: Occasionally, you can earn extra JPs through rewards from world activities or side quests, so be sure to use them wisely.
statsallocation.page3description3: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

saviorcrystals.title: Savior Crystals
saviorcrystals.description1: Your progress can only be \c[4]Saved\c[0] by interacting with these crystals or during specific key moments. When needed, you can \c[4]Load\c[0] your saves to resume from that point. Additionally, they can activate the \c[4]Waypoint System\c[0], allowing you to travel between towns and cities in the blink of an eye. There are two types of \c[4]Savior Crystals\c[0]: \c[1]Blue Crystals\c[0] and \c[6]Yellow Crystals\c[0].
saviorcrystals.page2description1: - \c[1]Blue Crystals\c[0]: Used to \c[4]Save\c[0] your game or activate the \c[4]Waypoint System\c[0]. These can commonly be found in towns, cities, or sometimes in the wild.
saviorcrystals.page2description2: - \c[6]Yellow Crystals\c[0]: These not only allow you to \c[4]Save\c[0] but also come with a \c[4]Healing System\c[0], which fully restores your entire party. They are typically located in specific fields or dungeons.
saviorcrystals.page3description1: For the \c[4]Waypoint System\c[0], it is only available through \c[1]Blue Crystals\c[0] in towns or cities. You must first interact with these crystals to register the locations. At certain key moments, the \c[4]Waypoint System\c[0] may be disabled.
saviorcrystals.page3description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

equipmentcraft.title: Equipment Crafting
equipmentcraft.description1: In the world of Valkadia, the best way to obtain powerful gear is by crafting it. Crafted equipment grants significant bonuses to your \c[4]Basic Parameters\c[0], far exceeding what shop-bought equipment offers.
equipmentcraft.description2: Craftable equipment includes \c[4]Weapons\c[0], \c[4]Armor\c[0], \c[4]Shields\c[0], \c[4]Accessories\c[0], and even \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0]. These items come in various rarities, ranging from \c[3]#{itemrarity.advanced}\c[0], \c[9]#{itemrarity.rare}\c[0], \c[27]#{itemrarity.myth}\c[0], all the way up to \c[17]#{itemrarity.legend}\c[0]. As expected, the materials needed also vary. Some can be purchased from shops, while others must be gathered from monsters in the wild.
equipmentcraft.page2description1: You’ll need to learn equipment recipes before you can craft anything. New recipes are usually available at the \c[4]#{placenames.synthesisshop}\c[0] (or \c[4]#{placenames.magicshop}\c[0], \c[4]#{placenames.church}es\c[0] for \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0]) in every town or city, so be sure to visit those frequently.
equipmentcraft.page3description1: To craft, simply talk to a shopkeeper at a \c[4]#{placenames.synthesisshop}\c[0] (or \c[4]#{placenames.magicshop}\c[0], \c[4]#{placenames.church}es\c[0] for \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0]). They’ll show you a list of all recipes you’ve learned. Choose the equipment you want, and if you have the required materials, you can craft it by paying a certain cost.
equipmentcraft.page3description2: Note that some high-rarity equipment may require a lower-tier version as part of the crafting ingredients, so be sure to check all material requirements carefully.
equipmentcraft.page3description3: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

itemshop.title: #{placenames.itemshop}
itemshop.description1: This is the \c[4]#{placenames.itemshop}\c[0], where you can stock up on \c[4]potions\c[0] and \c[4]consumables\c[0]. You can also sell unused items to the merchants here.
itemshop.description2: Remember to visit regularly. Some shops even sell potions that can cure \c[5]Negative Status Effects\c[0].

magicshop.morrigan1: So, you want me to learn some... uh... *magic* here at the \c[4]#{placenames.magicshop}\c[0]?
magicshop.lilith1: Of course not. Magic isn’t really your thing. Something more... brute-force suits you better.
magicshop.morrigan2: Ouch... That jab went straight for the heart...
magicshop.lilith2: Anyway, here you’ll meet the \c[4]#{npcnames.spellresearcher}\c[0]. They’ve got some solid \c[4]#{termwords.magic}\c[0] spells and powerful \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0] scrolls. You might actually find something useful.
magicshop.morrigan3: Feels weird. I always thought the techniques my father taught me would be more than enough.
magicshop.lilith3: You're a great fighter, no doubt. But trust me! Learning new things never hurts.
magicshop.morrigan4: Fine, fine... So what about that other guy over there?
magicshop.lilith5: \n[1]... Can you please be serious for once?
magicshop.lilith7: I think you'd better go talk to them. You’ll learn a lot more than just standing here staring and asking non-sense questions.

spellresearcher.title: Skill Learning
spellresearcher.description1: Learning new skills is one of the best ways to maximize your power in battle, as they offer many benefits to your characters.
spellresearcher.description2: Each character has a unique set of skills. To learn new ones, visit the \c[4]#{npcnames.spellresearcher}\c[0] at \c[4]#{placenames.magicshop}s\c[0] or \c[4]#{placenames.church}es\c[0] in major cities.
spellresearcher.page2description1: Different skills require different conditions, such as level, prerequisite skills, or cost. There are various types of skills: \c[4]#{termwords.magic}\c[0], \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0], \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0], \c[4]#{termwords.passive}\c[0], and even \c[4]#{termwords.ultimate}\c[0].
spellresearcher.page3description1: Before undergoing \c[4]Potential Awakening\c[0], a character can learn up to \c[3]08\c[0] \c[4]standard skills\c[0], including \c[4]#{termwords.magic}\c[0], \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0], \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0], and \c[4]#{termwords.passive}\c[0]. Of those, only \c[3]03\c[0] can be \c[4]#{termwords.passive}\c[0]. There is no limit on the number of active skills within the total. \c[4]#{termwords.ultimate}\c[0] skills are separate, and you can learn up to \c[3]01\c[0] of them.
spellresearcher.page3description2: Sometimes, equipment may grant additional skills. These do not count toward your learned skill slots and will disappear once the equipment is unequipped.
spellresearcher.page3description3: You can track the number of learned skills in your \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.status}\c[0] > \c[4]#{yepstatus.statinvestmentinfo}\c[0].
spellresearcher.page4description1: If you reach your skill limit, you can reset your learned skills by speaking with the \c[4]#{npcnames.skillmaster}\c[0] at any \c[4]#{placenames.magicshop}\c[0] or \c[4]#{placenames.church}\c[0] in major cities. The reset cost is \c[3]500\c[0] per \c[4]Standard Skill Learned\c[0], \c[3]2000\c[0] per \c[4]Ultimate Skill Learned\c[0], plus \c[3]100\c[0] per \c[4]Player’s Level\c[0] (\c[6]#{termwords.gold}\c[0]).
spellresearcher.page4description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].
spellresearcher.npc13: A \c[4]\ii[16]\c[0]! It lets you browse techniques remotely without having to visit me. Enjoy!
spellresearcher.morrigan11: This is seriously handy! Thanks a bunch!
spellresearcher.skipnpc1: That's the whole thing. You've got it all, right?
spellresearcher.skipmor1: Yeah. Seems easy enough. I'm confident with this!
spellresearcher.skipnpc2: So here, I have something for you. Take this gift!

gemmaker.title: #{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}
gemmaker.description1: \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0] enhance your equipment by embedding special effects into it. There are three types: \c[2]#{termwords.offensivecrystal}\c[0], \c[4]#{termwords.defensivecrystal}\c[0], and \c[6]#{termwords.supportingcrystal}\c[0]. Crystals also vary in rarity, from #{itemrarity.normal} to \c[3]#{itemrarity.advanced}\c[0], \c[9]#{itemrarity.rare}\c[0], and beyond. The higher the rarity, the more effects it carries.
gemmaker.page2description1: - \c[2]#{termwords.offensivecrystal}\c[0]: Infuse weapons with attack-based effects, known as \c[2]Reinforcement Runes\c[0]. Only usable on weapons.
gemmaker.page2description2: - \c[4]#{termwords.defensivecrystal}\c[0]: Add defense-based effects called \c[4]Reinforcement Glyphs\c[0]. These are exclusive to armor.
gemmaker.page2description3: - \c[6]#{termwords.supportingcrystal}\c[0]: Provide support-type effects. These have no special classification and can be slotted into any gear freely.
gemmaker.page3description1: To socket a \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0], open the \c[4]Menu\c[0], go to the \c[4]#{yepshopmenucore.equip}\c[0] section of a character, select \c[4]#{yepequipcustomize.customize}\c[0], then pick a piece of gear. You’ll see a list of compatible \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0]. Choose one to embed it.
gemmaker.page4description1: You can freely attach or remove \c[4]#{termwords.reinforcementcrystals}\c[0]. To remove one, repeat the same steps, and then select \c[4]#{yepattachaugments.removeaugment}\c[0] from the list. This will detach the current crystal from the equipment.
gemmaker.page4description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].
gemmaker.morrigan5: Thanks a bunch! I’ll put them to good use.
gemmaker.npc21: Good luck out there! And take care!
gemmaker.skipnpc1: That's everything. If you have any questions, I'll gladly answer.
gemmaker.skipmor1: I guess that's enough for me!
gemmaker.skipnpc2: I'm glad to hear that! Here, take this as my gift!

tool.title: #{msxinventory.tools}
tool.description1: \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] are special items that help you interact with certain objects in the environment. Sometimes, during your journey, you will need their assistance to continue, like when a boulder blocks your path, you might need a \c[4]\ii[28]\c[0] to break it. All \c[4]interactable objects\c[0] will display a \c[6]Yellow Exclamation Mark\c[0] (\c[6]!\c[0]) above them when you're nearby, indicating which objects can be interacted with.
tool.page2description1: To use \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0], you need to face or step on the \c[4]interactable objects\c[0], then look at the top-left corner of your screen. Your selected \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] will appear inside the \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools} button\c[0]. You can cycle through your \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] by pressing \c[4]Q\c[0] or \c[4]W\c[0], or simply use your \c[4]Mouse\c[0] to click the two arrows button. After selecting the correct \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0], hit the \c[4]Alt\c[0] button, or click on the \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] itself with your \c[4]Mouse\c[0].
tool.page3description1: Some objects do not require \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] to interact with, and you can interact with them directly. However, for objects that require \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0], using the wrong tool will either do nothing or cause a penalty.
tool.page3description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

selfcrafting.title: Self-Crafting
selfcrafting.description1: Sometimes, after collecting special materials in the wild, either by using \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] or simply picking them up, you may need to combine them into something new. This could be a new \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] or an item required to progress in the story. That’s where \c[4]Self-Crafting\c[0] comes into play.
selfcrafting.page2description1: To use \c[4]Self-Crafting\c[0], head to the \c[4]Menu\c[0], then select \c[4]#{menu.craft}\c[0]. There, you’ll find a list of all craftable items. Select the item you want to craft and check whether you have the required materials. Once all conditions are met, press \c[4]Z\c[0], \c[4]Space\c[0], or \c[4]Left Click\c[0] to confirm. Don’t forget to check the quantity before crafting.
selfcrafting.page3description1: It’s a good idea to check this feature regularly, as you may need to craft certain items throughout your journey to keep progressing.
selfcrafting.page3description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

equipmentmold.title: Equipment #{termwords.mold}s
equipmentmold.description1: Crafted equipment with rarity from #{itemrarity.rare} or higher always requires a certain amount of special materials called \c[4]#{termwords.mold}\c[0]. These are obtained by disassembling equipment with #{itemrarity.advanced} rarity or higher.
equipmentmold.page2description1: To obtain \c[4]#{termwords.mold}\c[0] from your equipment, head over to \c[4]#{menu.inventory}\c[0]. There, you will see categories of equipment such as \c[4]#{msxinventory.weapon}\c[0] and \c[4]#{msxinventory.armor}\c[0]. Disassembling either of these will produce the respective \c[4]#{termwords.mold}\c[0] types, such as weapons for weapon \c[4]#{termwords.mold}\c[0] and so on. Select an equipment, press \c[4]Z\c[0], \c[4]Space\c[0], or \c[4]Left Click\c[0] on it, then choose \c[4]#{yepitemdisassemble.disassemblecommand}\c[0]. Be sure to use \c[4]\ii[294]\c[0] for a successful disassembly. After the process, you will receive \c[4]#{termwords.mold}\c[0], ready to be used for crafting higher-rarity equipment.
equipmentmold.page3description1: Remember that \c[4]#{termwords.mold}s\c[0] have different rarities, just like equipment. Disassembling equipment will produce a \c[4]#{termwords.mold}\c[0] with the same rarity, which can be used as material for crafting equipment one rarity higher. For example, an \c[3]#{itemrarity.advanced} #{termwords.mold}\c[0] will be used as crafting material for #{itemrarity.rare} equipment, and so on.
equipmentmold.page3description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

breakableitem.title: #{msxinventory.breakable}
breakableitem.description1: Sometimes, you will obtain items that require disassembling to transform them into something more useful, especially when the disassembled items are \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0]. These are called \c[4]#{msxinventory.breakable}\c[0]. Once acquired, they will be placed in the \c[4]#{msxinventory.tools}\c[0] category within your \c[4]#{menu.inventory}\c[0].
breakableitem.page2description1: To disassemble the \c[4]#{msxinventory.breakable}\c[0], simply open your \c[4]Menu\c[0] and go to your \c[4]#{menu.inventory}\c[0], then \c[4]#{battletext.item}. Inside, you will find the \c[4]#{msxinventory.breakable}\c[0] category. All items in this category can be disassembled. Select the one you need and press \c[4]Z\c[0], \c[4]Space\c[0], or \c[4]Left Click\c[0] on it, then choose \c[4]#{yepitemdisassemble.disassemblecommand}\c[0]. Remember to use the \c[4]\ii[294]\c[0] to successfully disassemble it. Once completed, you will receive a new, useful item, ready for use.
breakableitem.page3description1: Sometimes, other items not categorized as \c[4]#{msxinventory.breakable}\c[0] can still be disassembled, including some \c[4]#{msxinventory.keyitems}\c[0]. They may provide hidden rewards, so be sure to check and carefully read the descriptions of all items.
breakableitem.page3description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

ship.title: Sail to the Ocean!
ship.description1: Sometimes, you will need to cross the ocean to reach other lands. Since traveling on foot is impossible, you must sail using your own ship.
ship.page2description1: To start sailing, find the #{npcnames.harbormaster} at the dock in certain towns or cities and speak to him. He will take you to your ship and allow you to begin sailing.
ship.page3description1: While onboard, use the \c[3]Direction Keys\c[0] to control your ship. To dock at an island or shoreline, face the land and press \c[3]Space\c[0], \c[3]Z\c[0], or use your \c[3]Mouse\c[0]. After docking, you can return to your ship by facing it and pressing \c[3]Space\c[0], \c[3]Z\c[0], or using your \c[3]Mouse\c[0] again.
ship.page4description1: To return to a town or city, locate the \c[2]Red Magic Circle\c[0]. Move your ship onto it, then press \c[3]Space\c[0], \c[3]Z\c[0], or use your \c[3]Mouse\c[0].
ship.page4description2: You can always read about this again in the \c[4]Menu\c[0] > \c[4]#{menu.gamemanual}\c[0].

sanguineoceanextended.title: #{mapnames.sanguineoceanextended}
sanguineoceanextended.description1: You have entered the #{mapnames.sanguineoceanextended}, which is connected to #{mapnames.sanguineocean}. You can now freely sail between the two areas with your ship.
sanguineoceanextended.page2description1: To travel between areas, look for the \c[17]Yellow Magic Circle\c[0] and move your ship onto it.
sanguineoceanextended.page3description1: You can now dock at either #{mapnames.seagullport} or #{mapnames.shiroitown}. Keep in mind that you can only sail again from the location where you docked your ship.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.desc: <WordWrap>All the sections below contain the basic information of the game. If you happen to miss a tutorial, you can find them again here.

basiccontrols.title: Basic Controls
basiccontrols.desc1: <WordWrap>\fs[23]Here’s a quick overview of the basic controls. You can play using either the \c[4]Keyboard\c[0] or the \c[4]Mouse\c[0].
basiccontrols.desc2:
\c[4]Arrow Keys\c[0] or \c[4]Mouse\c[0]: Move & Navigate
\c[4]Space\c[0], \c[4]Z\c[0], or \c[4]Left Click\c[0]: Confirm actions
\c[4]ESC\c[0], \c[4]X\c[0], or \c[4]Right Click\c[0]: Open Menu or Cancel
basiccontrols.desc3:
\c[4]Shift\c[0]: Dash or Skip dialogue
\c[4]Q\c[0], \c[4]E\c[0]: Switch characters in menu or cycle TOOLs
\c[4]Alt\c[0]: Use TOOLs or activate Auto-Conversation

basicfeatures.title: Basic Features
basicfeatures.desc: <WordWrap>This section covers the core features of the game. Here, you'll find information about how the game mechanics work. Select this entry to explore more detailed sub-entries.

basiccombat.desc1: <WordWrap>\fs[23]During battle, you can choose from several actions. Each one plays a key role in combat strategy:
basiccombat.desc2: \fs[23]\c[4]#{battletext.attack}\c[0]: Perform a basic attack. The damage dealt is based on your \c[4]#{termwords.atk} (ATK)\c[0] value.
basiccombat.desc3: \fs[23]\c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0]: Use physical-type skills. Some may also inflict negative status effects (debuffs).
basiccombat.desc4: \fs[23]\c[4]#{termwords.magic}\c[0]: Cast magical-type skills. Like \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0], some can apply debuffs to enemies.
basiccombat.desc5: \fs[23]\c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0]: Use supportive skills to heal HP, restore MP, or apply positive effects (buffs).
basiccombat.desc6: \fs[23]\c[4]#{termwords.ultimate}\c[0]: Unleash powerful skills with \c[4]#{generalskilldesc.certain}\c[0] effects. Use them wisely.
basiccombat.desc7: \fs[23]\c[4]#{battletext.guard}\c[0]: Brace for impact. Reduces incoming damage on that turn.
basiccombat.desc8: \fs[23]\c[4]#{battletext.item}\c[0]: Use consumable items from your inventory to recover, heal, or gain advantages in battle.
basiccombat.desc9: \fs[23]Pay attention to your characters' information panel: \c[2]Hit Points (HP)\c[0], \c[1]Mana Points (MP)\c[0], \c[3]Tension Points (TP)\c[0], and any \c[5]Status Effects\c[0]. If all party members’ HP reach 0, you will lose the battle. Stay alert and plan your moves carefully.

hittype.desc1: <WordWrap>\fs[22]Understanding hit types is key to mastering battle strategy. Each type has unique effects and interacts differently with defensive stats:
hittype.desc2: \fs[22]\c[10]#{generalskilldesc.physical}\c[0]: This hit type mostly deals physical damage, and is reduced by \c[4]#{termwords.def} (DEF)\c[0] and \c[4]#{termwords.pdr} (PDR)\c[0]. The accuracy of physical skills depends on \c[4]#{termwords.hit} (HIT)\c[0], and they can be dodged by raising \c[4]#{termwords.eva} (EVA)\c[0].
hittype.desc3: \fs[22]\c[9]#{generalskilldesc.magical}\c[0]: This hit type mostly deals magical damage, and is reduced by \c[4]#{termwords.mdf} (MDF)\c[0] and \c[4]#{termwords.mdr} (MDR)\c[0]. Magical skills have a high base accuracy (\c[4]100% + #{termwords.hit} (HIT)\c[0]) but can be avoided with \c[4]#{termwords.mev} (MEV)\c[0]. Some characters may even reflect magical damage if they have a high \c[4]#{termwords.mrf} (MRF)\c[0], completely nullifying the effect.
hittype.desc4: \fs[22]\c[14]#{generalskilldesc.certain}\c[0]: This hit type always hits and cannot be dodged or reflected. Its damage is usually reduced by either \c[4]#{termwords.def} (DEF)\c[0] or \c[4]#{termwords.mdf} (MDF)\c[0]. Certain-hit skills may also have special properties, like breaking \c[4]#{battletext.guard}\c[0] or ignoring all forms of defense.
hittype.desc5: \fs[22]Most \c[4]#{termwords.ability}\c[0] skills have \c[4]#{generalskilldesc.physical}\c[0], while most \c[4]#{termwords.magic}\c[0] skills have \c[4]#{generalskilldesc.magical}\c[0], unless otherwise stated. \c[4]#{generalskilldesc.certain}\c[0] is typically found in \c[4]#{termwords.aid}\c[0] and \c[4]#{termwords.ultimate}\c[0] skills.

damagetype.desc1: <WordWrap>\fs[23]There are two main types of damage you should understand, each with different mechanics and effects:
damagetype.desc2: \fs[23]\c[4]Direct Damage\c[0]: This type of damage is applied immediately when an attack or skill lands. It includes damage dealt, damage received, and healing effects.
damagetype.desc3: \fs[23]\c[4]Non-Direct Damage\c[0]: This type is applied at specific moments or comes from secondary effects, such as bonus damage from a skill, or effects caused by \c[5]Status Effects\c[0] and \c[4]Damage Over Time\c[0].
damagetype.desc4: \fs[23]\c[4]Damage Over Time\c[0] is a special case of \c[4]Non-Direct Damage\c[0] and is divided into two categories:
